$comment: IntentProof rule reason vocabulary. Each entry maps a stable reason code to its rule category and a short human description. Codes follow the convention <outcome>.<category>.<specifier> where outcome is one of pass | fail | inconclusive. The canonical rule grammar lives in schema/policy.v1.schema.json. This file is the source of truth for the reason vocabulary; schema/finding.v1.schema.json reason enum is checked by validate_reasons.ts to match this list exactly. Entries optionally carry richer authored copy: title, typical_causes, typical_owners, remediation_template, documentation_url. Codes listed in demo_blocker_reasons must populate the full set; validate_reasons.ts enforces this so the no-signup demo cannot render bespoke prose that diverges from production finding copy.

reasons.description: Required action occurred within the configured min/max bounds.
Required action did not occur at all in the matched scope.
reasons.title: Required step was skipped
typical_causes: The wrapped function for this step was never called on this code path.
The step ran but raised before recording its event (check upstream errors).
A correlation-id mismatch caused the event to be filed under a different flow.
The step is gated by a feature flag that was disabled in this environment.
typical_owners: the team that owns the missing action
reasons.remediation_template: Verify that ${action} runs on every successful ${flow_kind} flow. Check the call site for early returns, ensure it is inside the same correlation-id context as the preceding steps, and confirm any feature flag gating the call. If the step is intentionally optional, mark it as such in the policy.

reasons.description: Required action occurred but fewer times than the configured minimum.
Required action occurred more times than the configured maximum.
Forbidden action did not occur (or only occurred outside its gate).
Forbidden action occurred and no gating clause is configured.
Forbidden action occurred after its configured predecessor action.
reasons.title: Forbidden step ran after a step that should have terminated the flow
typical_causes: A retry path re-ran ${action} after the predecessor had already completed.
Two concurrent workers picked up the same correlation and both reached ${action}.
An idempotency key was missing or ignored at the ${action} call site.
Upstream cancellation did not propagate before ${action} fired.
typical_owners: the team that owns the forbidden action
the team that owns the predecessor action
reasons.remediation_template: Audit retries, concurrency, and idempotency around ${action}. Make ${action} reject calls that would follow ${predecessor_action} on the same correlation, or short-circuit before invoking ${action} when the predecessor has already settled. If the duplicate is operationally expected, refine the policy to scope the predecessor more precisely.

reasons.description: Forbidden action occurred without the required predecessor action.
All matched after-events were preceded by their before-event.
An after-event occurred earlier than its paired before-event within scope.
Pair-scoped ordering rule saw an after-event with no matching before-event.
Generic ordering violation when a more specific reason does not apply.
After-event is present but no before-event exists anywhere in the flow.
Action count satisfies the configured exactly / min / max bounds.
Action count does not equal the configured exactly value.
Action count is below the configured minimum.
Action count exceeds the configured maximum.
Action count falls outside the configured range when min/max share a single specifier.
Interval between the configured anchors falls within the allowed window.
to-anchor occurred before from-anchor beyond the clock-skew tolerance.
Interval is shorter than the configured minimum.
Interval is longer than the configured maximum.
Generic temporal-window violation when a more specific reason does not apply.
from-anchor or to-anchor event is absent from the flow.
Negative interval is within the configured clock-skew tolerance.
Temporal max (or min) duration in the rule is missing or not parseable as an ISO-8601 duration, so the window cannot be evaluated.
Configured consensus threshold met with no live disagreement.
Insufficient agreeing sources to satisfy the configured threshold.
Two or more live sources actively disagree on the claim value.
reasons.title: Internal and external sources disagree about what happened
typical_causes: An action was taken in ${external_source} (for example via that provider's own dashboard) without going through the wrapped code path, so the application never recorded an event.
The wrapped call succeeded but the corresponding event was dropped before reaching the platform (network loss, outbox eviction).
${external_source} delivered a stale or replayed webhook that no longer matches current state.
A correlation-id mismatch placed the external attestation under a different flow than the internal events.
typical_owners: the team that owns ${claim}'s execution path
reasons.remediation_template: Compare the internal evidence (${internal_evidence_ref}) with the external attestation (${external_evidence_ref}) for claim ${claim}. If the external action was taken out-of-band, document the operational reason and tighten access to ${external_source}; if the internal path failed silently, restore the missing event from logs and investigate why the wrapped call did not record it.

reasons.description: Generic consensus shortfall when a more specific reason does not apply.
All external sources unavailable and no internal source exists.
Required signature missing or invalid while require_signed_sources is true.
Claim value falls within the configured numeric bound.
Claim value violates the configured numeric bound (lt/lte/gt/gte/eq/neq).
Configured claim was not emitted by the named source for the flow.
Claim value equals the configured expected value.
Claim value differs from the configured expected value.
Configured claim was not emitted by the named source for the flow.
Required source signature missing or invalid while require_signed is true.
Legacy reserved code; verifiers should use inconclusive.unknown.unsupported_rule_category for unrecognized rule category strings.
Rule category is not a known policy DSL kind (malformed policy or verifier mismatch); see rule_category on the finding.
After-action from the rule was not found in the flow.
Consensus rule is missing a claim identifier in spec.
Value bound rule uses an operator this verifier does not implement.
Value bound rule has a non-numeric bound or otherwise invalid bound spec.
Claim match rule is missing expected_value in spec.
claim_match rule was not evaluated because this verifier runtime has no handler for that category.
value_bound rule was not evaluated because this verifier runtime has no handler for that category.
